name: Pear Therapeutics
tagline: Pioneer of FDA-approved prescription digital therapeutics
description: Pear raised $400M and went public at $1.6B to build FDA-approved digital therapeutics apps. Revenue of $12.7M vs $136M expenses led to bankruptcy, with assets sold for just $6M.
story: Pear Therapeutics was founded in 2013 as a pioneer in prescription digital therapeutics — software-based treatments prescribed by doctors and cleared by the FDA. The company went public via SPAC in 2021 at a $1.6 billion valuation, having raised approximately $400 million. Pear had multiple FDA-cleared products for substance abuse and insomnia, but couldn't solve the fundamental challenge: getting doctors to prescribe apps and insurers to pay for them. Revenue reached only $12.7 million against $136 million in expenses in 2022. In April 2023, Pear filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy, terminating 92% of its workforce — 170 employees. At auction, the company's assets sold for a total of just $6 million, a stunning markdown from its billion-dollar valuation.

sources.title: Pear Therapeutics assets sold for $6M at auction
